name: El Tello Municipal Natural Park Loop
name_original: El Paraje Natural de El Tello — Vuelta desde Alginet
description: Cycle 25.8 miles (41.5 km) through El Paraje Natural de El Tello from Alginet, a moderate route with diverse scenery and viewpoints.
region_intro: Touring cycling around Alginet offers diverse landscapes, transitioning between flat terrain and low mountain sections. The region is situated in the Valencian Community of Spain, providing varied routes for cyclists. Notable natural features include the El Tello Municipal Natural Park and proximity to the Albufera Natural Park, which features a large freshwater lagoon and extensive rice fields. The area also includes traditional orange groves and borders the Falaguera Mountain Range. Best touring cycling routes around Alginet The most popular touring cycling route is El Tello Municipal Natural Park Loop, a 25.8 miles (41.5 km) trail that takes 3 hours 5 minutes to complete. This route features varied landscapes and panoramic viewpoints within the El Tello Municipal Natural Park. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Umbracle – Albufera Viewpoint loop from Benifaió - Almussafes, a moderate 38.9 miles (62.6 km) path. This route offers scenic rides through the unique wetland ecosystem of the Albufera Natural Park. Local touring cyclists also love the Albufera Viewpoint – Palmar Road loop from Benifaió - Almussafes, a 24.6 miles (39.7 km) trail leading through the Albufera Natural Park, often completed in about 2 hours 20 minutes. Touring cycling around Alginet is defined by flat sections, low mountain terrain, traditional orange groves, and the unique wetlands of Albufera Natural Park. The network offers options for various fitness levels, from moderate to challenging. The routes in Alginet are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 100 reviews. More than 1900 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Alginet's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Alginet?
A: There are over 190 touring cycling routes around Alginet, offering a wide range of options for different skill levels and preferences.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes near Alginet?
A: The terrain around Alginet is quite diverse, featuring a mix of flat sections, low mountain areas, and traditional orange groves. You'll find routes that traverse the unique wetlands of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31759958" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Albufera Natural Park&lt;/a&gt;, as well as more challenging paths with varied landscapes and panoramic viewpoints within the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e917600473/el-tello-municipal-natural-park-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Tello Municipal Natural Park&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes in the Alginet area?
A: Yes, many touring cycling routes around Alginet are circular. For example, the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e917600473/el-tello-municipal-natural-park-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Tello Municipal Natural Park Loop&lt;/a&gt; and the scenic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38230405" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Albufera Viewpoint – Palmar Road loop&lt;/a&gt; are both circular, allowing you to start and finish at the same point.

Q: What are the best times of year for touring cycling in Alginet?
A: Alginet benefits from consistently pleasant weather year-round, making it suitable for cycling in most seasons. Spring and autumn generally offer the most comfortable temperatures for longer rides, though winter cycling is also possible due to the mild climate.

Q: What natural attractions can I see along the touring cycling routes?
A: Many routes pass through or near significant natural areas. You can explore the varied landscapes of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e917600473/el-tello-municipal-natural-park-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Tello Municipal Natural Park&lt;/a&gt;, known for its panoramic vistas. The region is also close to the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31759958" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Albufera Natural Park&lt;/a&gt;, a vital wetland with a large freshwater lagoon and extensive rice fields. You might also encounter traditional orange groves and the Falaguera Mountain Range.

Q: Are there any viewpoints along the cycling routes in Alginet?
A: Yes, several routes offer impressive viewpoints. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e917600473/el-tello-municipal-natural-park-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Tello Municipal Natural Park Loop&lt;/a&gt; includes the Alto del Besori, which provides panoramic vistas. Other elevated spots in the Alginet area, such as 'Los Lagos' and 'Altos de Valencia', offer expansive views of the Valencian coast, stretching from Monte Picayo to Montgó.

Q: What historical or cultural landmarks can I explore while cycling near Alginet?
A: While primarily an outdoor destination, Alginet offers several cultural and historical sites. You can find historical towers like 'Luengo' and 'Borrero' among the orange groves. The town itself features &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4489764" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hermitage of Saint Joseph&lt;/a&gt; and a 16th-century castle, Cabanyelles Castle, which is now part of the town hall. The Parish Church, built between 1654 and 1699, is also notable.

Q: Are there any family-friendly touring cycling routes in Alginet?
A: Alginet offers routes suitable for various fitness levels, including moderate options that can be enjoyed by families. Routes through flatter sections, particularly those near the Albufera Natural Park, tend to be less demanding. Always check the route's difficulty and elevation profile on komoot to ensure it matches your family's capabilities.

Q: What do other touring cyclists say about the routes in Alginet?
A: The touring cycling routes around Alginet are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 100 reviews. Cyclists often praise the diverse landscapes, the mix of flat and low mountain terrain, and the scenic beauty of areas like El Tello Municipal Natural Park and Albufera Natural Park.

Q: Are there options for longer, more challenging touring cycling routes?
A: Yes, Alginet caters to more advanced touring cyclists with several challenging routes. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/17301341" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hort de Feliu Park loop from Alginet&lt;/a&gt; is rated as difficult, featuring significant elevation gain. Another demanding option is the extensive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38281844" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Plaza de Toros de Valencia – Valencia Cathedral loop from Alginet&lt;/a&gt;, which covers a considerable distance and elevation.

Q: Where can I find parking for cycling routes in Alginet?
A: Parking is generally available in and around Alginet, particularly near the starting points of popular routes or in the town center. Many routes are accessible from various points, allowing you to choose a convenient parking spot. Specific parking details are often available within individual komoot tour descriptions.

Q: Can I reach the cycling routes by public transport?
A: Alginet is connected by public transport, which can facilitate access to the cycling routes. The region is part of the broader Valencia area, which has a bike-friendly infrastructure. For detailed information on public transport options and how to transport your bike, you may consult resources like &lt;a href="https://www.visitvalencia.com/en/what-to-do-valencia/valencia-by-bike" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Visit Valencia's cycling guide&lt;/a&gt;.

Alto del Besori is a mountain located in the Ribera Alta region of the Valencian Community. It is part of the Paraje Natural Municipal del Tello and is known for its impressive panoramic views.
The route to Alto del Besori is popular with hikers and offers a varied landscape that includes ravines and pine forests. The route is moderate in difficulty and is suitable for most hikers with some experience.

A panoramic point from where you will undoubtedly have the best views of the entire Albufera Natural Park, Valencia and the Levante coast in the Cullera and Tavernes area. Tranquility also reigns so take a few minutes to enjoy this beautiful place.

The transfer channel offers a route that passes through various towns and villages in the interior, connecting Valencia with Tous. It is in good condition, with a paved surface and practically no slope, ideal for rolling!

faqs.question: Where can I park to start this cycling route?
faqs.answer: The route starts and returns to Alginet. You can typically find parking within the town of Alginet, which serves as the convenient access point for this touring bicycle route into El Paraje Natural de El Tello.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this cycling route?
faqs.answer: This route is predominantly paved, which is ideal for touring bicycles. However, be aware that while mostly paved, some unpaved sections are possible within the wider El Tello Natural Park area, which could add a challenging element for touring bikes not equipped for rougher terrain. The landscape features varied terrain including ravines and pine forests.

faqs.question: Is this cycling route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: This route is classified as moderate and requires a good level of physical fitness. While some paths in the broader El Tello Natural Park are easier, this specific route is best suited for cyclists with some experience rather than absolute beginners.

faqs.question: What points of interest or viewpoints can I expect to see along the way?
faqs.answer: Along the route, you'll encounter several notable spots. These include the Catholic and Social Center, and several viewpoints offering picturesque views: L'Estepar Viewpoint, Besori Viewpoint, and Alts de Valencia Viewpoint. You'll also pass by the Júcar - Túria Water Transfer.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The El Tello Natural Park area offers beautiful scenery throughout much of the year. Spring and autumn generally provide the most pleasant cycling conditions with milder temperatures. Summers can be hot, so if cycling then, it's best to start early in the morning to avoid the midday heat.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for cycling in El Tello Natural Park?
faqs.answer: No, there are typically no specific permits or entrance fees required to cycle this route within the Paratge Natural Municipal El Tello. It is a natural park open for public enjoyment.

faqs.question: Is this cycling route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the natural park itself is generally accessible, this route is designed for touring bicycles. If you plan to bring a dog, ensure it can comfortably keep pace with a touring bicycle for over 40 km, or consider alternative, shorter, and less technical routes within the park that might be more suitable for pets.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other cycling paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route intersects with or runs alongside several other paths for short sections. These include parts of the Serra d'Alèdua, Pont d’Alfarp, Senda de l'Estepar, Senda Estepar, Camí de la Lloma del Comte, Senda Pla de l'Estepar, and Senda Pujà de Potan.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery can I expect while cycling?
faqs.answer: You can expect picturesque views encompassing both inland areas and glimpses of the coastline. The route takes you through the enchanting El Tello Municipal Natural Park, featuring mountains, pine forests, scrubland, and dryland crops, offering a diverse natural environment.

faqs.question: What should I consider regarding my bike choice for this route?
faqs.answer: Given that the route is mostly paved but may include some unpaved sections, a touring bicycle is suitable. However, if your touring bike has wider tires or is more robust, it will handle any potential rougher patches more comfortably. Be prepared for varied surfaces.

faqs.question: Is there public transport available to reach the starting point in Alginet?
faqs.answer: Alginet is a town with public transport connections, making it accessible. You can check local bus or train schedules to reach Alginet, though specific transport options for carrying bicycles may vary and should be confirmed in advance.
